replies.msg:
2 hrs left

No deal leave

The latest from Westminster as the prime minister warns MPs that Brexit might be "lost" if they reject her deal for a second time.

Key points:

May says Brexit 'might be lost' if her deal is rejected in vote due around 7pm

PM set for defeat as DUP and bloc of Tory MPs say they can't back deal

May says she got 'legally binding' changes to backstop - but attorney general says risk of being stuck in it 'remains unchanged'

Labour's Jeremy Corbyn says 'not one single word' of deal has changed
deal ng deal~

Brexit deal vote: What do I need to know?

On Tuesday, MPs will pass their verdict on Prime Minister Theresa May's plan to take Britain out of the European Union on 29 March. Here is what you need to know about the vote.

When will the vote be held?

On Tuesday evening, after a full day of debate. We won't know the exact time until the government announces it.

What are MPs voting on?

They are passing judgement on the deal Theresa May spent two years negotiating with the European Union.
Brexit: What could happen next?

Why is the vote important?

This is the big one.

If MPs vote the deal down, one of two things is likely to happen.

The UK will either leave the EU on 29 March without a withdrawal agreement - or that departure date will be delayed.

If they back the deal, the UK will leave the EU on 29 March but things will stay broadly as they are until December 2020, while the two sides thrash out a permanent trade deal.

Haven't MPs already rejected May's exit plan?
Yes - and by a massive margin.

So what has changed?

Mrs May said she secured "legally binding" changes to her Brexit deal on Monday, a day ahead of MPs voting on it.

She said the first change could be used to start a "formal dispute" against the EU if it tried to keep the UK tied into the backstop - the safety net designed to maintain an open border on the island of Ireland - indefinitely.

The second is a "joint statement" from the EU and the UK. It adds to the political declaration on the UK and EU's future relationship and commits to replacing the backstop with alternative arrangements by December 2020.

Attorney General Geoffrey Cox, the government's chief law officer, has published updated legal advice on the prime minister's deal, saying the new provisions secured by Mrs May "reduce the risk" of the UK being "indefinitely and involuntarily" held in the backstop.

But his advice adds that "the legal risk remains unchanged" that the UK would have no legal means of exiting without EU agreement.
What happens if MPs reject the deal again?
If MPs vote down Mrs May's withdrawal agreement on Tuesday, then MPs have been promised a vote on whether the UK should leave without a deal or not.

This is likely to take place on Wednesday.
If they back a no-deal Brexit in this vote, then the UK will leave without a deal on 29 March.
If they reject a no-deal Brexit then they could get a vote on Thursday on whether to request a delay to Brexit from the EU.

Will that be the final vote before Brexit?
Not necessarily.
If Mrs May loses the vote on Tuesday by a narrow margin, she could ask the EU for a short technical delay to allow everyone to regroup and make one last push to get it "over the line".
Mrs May could make that final push at the next EU summit on 22 March, with another Commons vote to follow, assuming she can get a delay to Brexit.

一走睇下愛爾蘭邊界點玩

quote.msg: 一走睇下愛爾蘭邊界點玩

replies.msg: Geoffrey Cox好似特登玩鳩May姐咁

原來bbc有中文ver.

英國議會周二將就首相特蕾莎·梅與歐盟達成的脫歐協議再次投票表決。事關英國究竟是協議脫歐、無協議脫歐還是延遲脫歐，因此萬眾矚目。

表決之前，英國首相特雷莎·梅表示，與歐盟緊急會談後，已經獲得了「有法律約束力」的協議修改。

梅首相透露，這些修改意謂著愛爾蘭邊界協議——即確保在英國和愛爾蘭之間避免出現邊檢的協議，不會是「永久性」的安排。

然而特雷莎·梅所稱的協議突破，並沒有打消各方的疑慮。英國總檢察長喬夫利·考克斯（Geoffrey Cox）周二表示，英國受制於歐盟條款的風險「仍然不變」。

考克斯的此番講話預計將對當天晚上的議員表決造成相當大的影響。

英國脫歐進程關鍵時刻，有必要了解以下基本的情況：

議會何時投票？

英國議會當地時間周二（3月12日）將全天展開討論，晚間7點開始投票，預計晚間9點將有結果。
(GMT+8 = 05:00)

就什麼問題投票？

議員將投票表決首相在過去兩年時間裏與歐盟達成的脫歐協議。

本次投票重要嗎？

非常重要！

如果議員否決脫歐協議，可能有以下兩種局面：英國要麼在3月29日無協議脫歐，要麼延遲脫歐。

如果議員通過脫歐協議，英國將在3月29日脫離歐盟，但是一切都將基本照常，要等到2020年12月英國與歐盟達成永久貿易協議之後才有實質變化。
英國真係煩膠的極致

Most imports tariff-free under no-deal plan http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/business-47551266

政府夾鳩硬嚟
噉就咩都唔洗過啦
How will the Irish border system work?

The government also announced that it will not introduce any new checks or controls, or require customs declarations for nearly all goods moving from across the border from Ireland to Northern Ireland in the event the UK leaves the EU without a deal.

The decision to drop all checks to avoid friction at the UK's land border with the EU will be temporary while longer term solutions are negotiated and was taken to recognise what the government described as "the unique social political and economic circumstances of Northern Ireland.
The government said tariffs will be payable on goods moving from the EU into the rest of the UK via Northern Ireland.
It insisted that this would create no border down the Irish sea because there would be no checks on goods moved between Northern Ireland and Britain.

點賣國法？

挪威瑞士呢啲國家都係take EU rules，唔會因為咁佢地就唔係獨立國家。唔想做rule taker一係就留係eu，一係就唔理北愛問題唔理對經濟同外交的傷害no deal離場。政治最基本就係唔可以脫離現實，而家英國用左兩年半都睇唔清個現實。

replies.msg:
佢最好畀英國extend
英國個no deal plan根本係玩嘢
佢單方面唔整關口喺北愛，同時又唔整關口喺北愛到不列顛島
唔收愛爾蘭去北愛嘅關稅，同時又收愛爾蘭同其他歐盟國家嘅關稅
變相佢不甘整一個大漏洞出嚟縮骨
喺冇簽任何deal底下，英國可以自由唔跟歐盟規矩，長遠嚟講啫係佢可以借北愛個漏洞嚟引啲唔合歐盟標準嘅嘢入愛爾蘭，又或者其他國家可以喺北愛到入歐盟單一市場而唔洗畀歐盟嘅關稅
歐盟要堵塞依個問題就只可以靠叫愛爾蘭做醜人，起個關口喺北愛。一係就隔開愛爾蘭，由愛爾蘭入嘅嘢都要清關。因為英國同歐盟冇簽協議，基本上英國係唔洗再聽歐盟講都得
最重要係就算英國話個no deal plan唔係永久，都一定係永久，因為佢地一樣唔會主動放咗關口喺北愛，北愛個漏洞只會一直存在直頭歐盟單一市場崩潰
